surface_symptom:
Two skfem catalog templates (navier_stokes::2d and hyperelasticity::2d) had been carrying the same family of bugs for months. Running the catalog's actual emit-and-run template raised:
  ValueError: could not broadcast input array from shape (2,N) into shape (M,)
inside skfem.assembly.form.bilinear_form._assemble, before any iteration could start. The bugs cascaded — even after the immediate ValueError, the next assembly call surfaced a fresh shape mismatch. An LLM following the catalog verbatim would write the script, run it, and dead-end on a stack trace from inside skfem's internals — with no Signal: clue from the catalog Pitfalls section. Layer-F coverage of these two templates had been deferred for multiple iterations because the rewrites were judged 'too complex for one batch'.
root_cause:
Both templates were written against an older skfem API (likely v6 or v8) that exposed previous-iterate displacement-gradient components as scalar w-kwargs:
  w['dux_dx'], w['dux_dy'], w['duy_dx'], w['duy_dy']
This interface was removed in skfem v12. The current interface is `basis.interpolate(u_prev_dofs)` returning a DiscreteField, passed as a single kwarg `u_prev` and accessed as `w['u_prev'].grad` (rank-2 tensor, shape (d, d, n_elem, n_quad)). The old kernels also used `u.grad[0]` / `v.grad[0]` flat-index access on ElementVector trial/test functions — which silently returned a (d, n_basis_per_elem) array per quadrature point, breaking the assembler's expected scalar-per-(elem, quad) contraction. Three additional cascading bugs: scalar laplace assembled on a vector basis (form returns wrong shape); intorder mismatch between mixed bases (asm raises Quadrature mismatch); MeshTri.init_sqsymmetric() lacks named boundaries (.with_boundaries() must attach 'top'/'left'/'right'/'bottom').
pitfall_db_entries: [API] In skfem 12, @BilinearForm and @LinearForm kernels receive previous-iterate fields via the DiscreteField API, NOT scalar w-kwargs. The contract is: outer loop calls `f = basis.interpolate(u_prev_dofs)` then `asm(form, basis, u_prev=f)`. Inside the kernel, access components as `w['u_prev'].value[i]` and gradients as `w['u_prev'].grad[i, j]`. Signal: passing per-component scalar kwargs (`w['dux_dx']` etc.) raises 'could not broadcast input array from shape (2,N) into shape (M,)' on the first assembly call.

[API] On ElementVector(P1) or ElementVector(P2), `u.grad[i]` and `v.grad[i]` indexing returns the wrong-shape array — skfem treats `grad(u)` of a vector field as a rank-2 tensor with components (d, d, n_elem, n_quad). Use skfem.helpers.grad(u)[i, j] for component access, or skfem.helpers.ddot(grad(u), grad(v)) for the vector Laplacian contraction. Signal: '(d, n_basis) into (n_dofs,)' broadcast error from skfem.assembly.form.bilinear_form._assemble.

[API] skfem.models.poisson.laplace is a SCALAR Laplacian — asm(laplace, vector_basis) errors. For a vector field, define a custom @BilinearForm using ddot(grad(u), grad(v)) from skfem.helpers. Signal: 'could not broadcast input array from shape (d, n_basis_per_elem) into shape (n_total_per_elem*n_quad,)' on the first asm() call for the viscous stiffness.

[API] skfem matrix multiplication: the @ operator does NOT work on skfem's (d, d, n_elem, n_quad)-shape tensor fields. matmul tries the last-two-dims convention and aborts with '(size N is different from d)'. Fall back to explicit component algebra (Finv[0,0]*Finv[0,0] + Finv[0,1]*Finv[0,1] for (Finv·Finv^T)[0,0]), or use the symmetric-tensor helpers ddot/dot/transpose. Signal: 'matmul: Input operand 1 has a mismatch in its core dimension 0' from numpy's @ inside the kernel.

[Numerical] Newton iteration on a Neo-Hookean Energy with too-aggressive default load (E=1.0 / traction=0.1 → ~10% relative load) drives the deformation gradient J = det(F) negative in some quadrature points after the first iter; log(J) returns NaN; the iteration diverges to NaN-everywhere displacements and exit code is STILL 0. Layer-F gates that only check rc==0 / no-Traceback pass silently. Fix: pick stiffness × load combinations that keep first-iter strain < 1% (e.g. E=1000 / traction=1.0 = 0.4% strain), or use load stepping with N steps of α=k/N. The catalog_template_executes fixture now additionally forbids 'Max displacement: nan' / '||du|| = nan' in output so silent-NaN regressions are caught.
agent_detection_after_fix:
Three new gates land:
  1. scripts/tier2_fixtures/cross_backend/catalog_template_executes/source.py — the Layer-F end-to-end gate now exercises skfem::navier_stokes::2d AND skfem::hyperelasticity::2d. Both must rc=0 in the repo .venv, and the fixture's forbid_in_output list now includes NaN sentinels ('Max displacement: nan', 'Max velocity magnitude: nan', '||du|| = nan', 'max(u) = nan') so a regression that reintroduces silent-NaN fails the gate even with rc=0.
  2. Layer-F coverage now 122/122 (100%) — any new skfem template variant that fails generation or execution is caught at the same gate.
  3. The 5 pitfall_db_entries above are linked from the catalog Pitfalls section of both physics, so the pre-execution critic surfaces them when the agent plans a Newton-iterated skfem solve. The post-execution critic matches against the 'could not broadcast' / 'matmul: Input operand' / 'Max displacement: nan' Signal: clauses when reading skfem stderr/stdout.
